personal.name: Champa Jha
personal.bio: I believe the best way to learn is by building. I'm passionate about exploring new technologies, turning ideas into reality, and creating software that delivers real value. Currently studying B.Tech. in Information Technology.

projects.title: Indian Sign Language Gen
projects.desc: An AI-based multimodal translation platform that converts English and Hindi speech, text, and visual content into Indian Sign Language (ISL), and translates ISL back into English and Hindi.
projects.vividDesc: A cutting-edge multimodal translation system developed to bridge the communication gap between the hearing-impaired community and the general public. This AI-powered tool accepts inputs in the form of spoken Hindi/English speech, written text, or video feeds, and translates them in real-time to Indian Sign Language (ISL) animations. It also acts as a bidirectional translator, taking ISL sign gestures from a camera feed and translating them back into audio and text.
features: Real-time conversion of speech to ISL animations.
Bidirectional camera translation from ISL back to speech.
Interactive tutorial modules for learning key ISL signs.
Advanced hand-gesture recognition using computer vision.

projects.title: Naye Pankh
projects.desc: A NGO management platform for volunteer registration, donation campaigns, and event management.
projects.vividDesc: An intuitive NGO management and fundraising ecosystem designed to streamline volunteer engagement and digital donations for 'Naye Pankh Foundation'. The portal features a secure custom authentication system, a real-time volunteer onboarding flow, administrative tools to launch donation drives, and a public-facing blog tracking social impact initiatives.
features: Dynamic volunteer onboarding registration and background approval.
Secure payments integration for digital donation campaigns.
Real-time announcements and blogging framework to document social impacts.
Comprehensive administrator panel for volunteer coordination and drive tracking.

projects.title: E-Swachh
projects.desc: A civic issue reporting platform that enables citizens to report and track municipal problems such as garbage accumulation, potholes, water leakage, and streetlight failures using real-time geolocation and image uploads.
projects.vividDesc: A geolocated civic complaint and sanitation management software. Citizens can flag public waste dumps, flooding, and sanitation issues by snapping a photo. The app automatically extracts GPS coordinates and creates an interactive pin on a live map. Municipal admins use the backend dashboard to triage issues, assign field workers, and update issue statuses in real time.
features: Interactive mapping with geolocated pinpoints and custom leaflet layers.
Photo-based reporting with EXIF metadata parsing for verification.
Role-based control rooms for government triage and field response.
Real-time civic status trackers for citizens to monitor complaint progress.

projects.title: One Piece
projects.desc: A pirate adventure game where users can explore the One Piece world and find treasure. Set sail into the Grand Line through an immersive One Piece-inspired web experience featuring cinematic animations, iconic characters, and a modern interactive UI built with React, Vite, and Framer Motion.
projects.vividDesc: One Piece follows Monkey D. Luffy, a young pirate whose body gained the properties of rubber after eating the Gum-Gum Fruit. Inspired by the legendary pirate Red-Haired Shanks, Luffy sets sail to find the legendary treasure known as the One Piece and become the King of the Pirates. During his journey across the Grand Line and the New World, he forms the Straw Hat Pirates, each member chasing their own dream while facing powerful enemies, uncovering hidden history, and challenging the oppressive World Government.
features: Stunning interactive character bios with animations.
Rich thematic custom backgrounds and stylized UI patterns.
Interactive Grand Line map route navigation.
Fluid page transitions mimicking comic book panel zooms.

projects.title: Selfie Shuffle
projects.desc: A fun and interactive web-based puzzle game where players transform their own selfies into a personalized jigsaw puzzle.
projects.vividDesc: A gamified web experience where users can take a live selfie using their web camera or upload any portrait image to instantly generate a custom, playable jigsaw puzzle. The game splits the image into canvas fragments using custom grid algorithms, shuffles them with interactive transition animations, and tracks player moves, time elapsed, and high scores.
features: Dynamic canvas image fragmentation splitting algorithm.
Interactive puzzle-piece dragging with grid snapping detection.
Local Storage database integrations for high-score tracking.
Camera streaming utilizing native browser WebRTC media APIs.

education.title: B.Tech. in Information Technology
education.institution: University College of Engineering and Technology, Hazaribagh

education.title: Matriculation
education.institution: St. Joseph's Convent Higher Secondary School, Chittaranjan

certifications.title: PROGRAMMING IN JAVA (ELITE)
certifications.description: Successfully completed the NPTEL certification course in Programming in Java with an Elite grade. The course covered advanced Java concepts including Object-Oriented Programming, Exception Handling, Multithreading, Generics, Applets, Swing, and Database Connectivity (JDBC).

certifications.title: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
certifications.description: Completed an intensive 4-week virtual internship in Artificial Intelligence at Codsoft. During this internship, I designed, developed, and deployed multiple machine learning solutions, including a Movie Recommendation System using collaborative filtering, an NLP-based Spam SMS Classifier, and a Tic-Tac-Toe AI bot leveraging the Minimax algorithm with alpha-beta pruning.

certifications.title: INTRODUCTION TO AI
certifications.description: Completed the Introduction to AI curriculum offered by Cisco Networking Academy, gaining core insights into the foundational theories of neural networks, deep learning models, data preprocessing pipelines, and ethical frameworks for building responsible AI applications.

achievements: Earned Elite status in NPTEL's Programming in Java certification, representing top performance.
Engineered and deployed E-Swachh, a civic complaint platform featuring custom Leaflet mapping and EXIF data coordination.
Integrated hardware-accelerated computer-vision frameworks inside Indian Sign Language Gen for fluid sign recognition.
accomplishments: Successfully pivoted from Civil Engineering (Diploma) into computer software science (B.Tech IT), adapting swiftly to OOPs, web paradigms, and AI architectures.
Completed virtual internships with Codsoft, Cisco, and Techies Gateway, delivering production-ready web interfaces.
awards: Academic Merit and Top Performer Recognition at UCET Hazaribagh

careerGoals.shortTerm: Secure a challenging role as a Full-Stack Developer or AI Engineer, focusing on building user-centric, interactive web applications and integrating advanced LLM capabilities.
careerGoals.longTerm: Architect accessibility-focused software platforms that utilize computer vision, real-time geodata, and speech models to solve real-world problems and bridge digital accessibility gaps.

hobbies: Studying layouts, animations, and typography structures (UI design and Vibe Coding).
Reading books, face painting, upcycling everyday materials into creative crafts, and exploring DIY art projects..
Solving canvas grid fragmentation logic and algorithmic puzzle games.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What is your greatest strength?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I am a self-taught, hands-on builder. I learn best by designing and coding functional apps. I love taking complex, dry logic and turning it into an interactive UI—like the custom sliding fragment algorithm in Selfie Shuffle or Leaflet maps in E-Swachh.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What is your biggest technical challenge so far?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: When building Indian Sign Language Gen, implementing real-time camera-based gesture recognition in the browser was challenging due to frame rate drops. I optimized it by writing lightweight frame throttling and using hardware-accelerated transforms, which made translation buttery smooth.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: Why B.Tech IT after Diploma in Civil Engineering?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: During my Civil Engineering diploma, I realized I wanted to build solutions in the digital space where iteration is fast and impact is immediate. I transitioned to IT to merge civil structural problem-solving logic with cutting-edge software development.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What technologies do you want to learn next?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I am deeply curious about advanced AI-Agent frameworks (like LangChain or AutoGen) and voice cloning integrations. I hope to build more voice-enabled and multi-agent applications.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: Tell me about yourself.
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I am an Information Technology student who enjoys building web applications that solve real-world problems. I learn best through projects and have worked on applications involving AI, accessibility, mapping, and responsive web development.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: Why do you enjoy software development?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I enjoy software development because it allows me to turn ideas into products that people can actually use. Seeing an application evolve from a simple concept into a functional solution is what motivates me.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What type of projects interest you the most?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I enjoy projects that combine problem-solving with user experience, especially those involving AI, accessibility, automation, and interactive web technologies.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: How do you learn new technologies?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I usually start by understanding the fundamentals, then build small projects to apply what I've learned. If I encounter challenges, I refer to documentation, community discussions, and experiment until I understand the solution.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: How do you approach debugging?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I break the problem into smaller parts, identify where the issue originates, and test each assumption systematically. I rely on browser developer tools, logging, and documentation to isolate and resolve bugs.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What motivates you to keep building projects?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: Every project teaches me something new. I enjoy challenging myself with unfamiliar technologies because each completed project improves both my technical skills and confidence.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: Which project are you most proud of?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: One of the projects I'm most proud of is Indian Sign Language Gen because it combines accessibility with AI and required integrating real-time camera input, gesture recognition, and an intuitive user interface.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: Describe a time you overcame a technical challenge.
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: While building one of my projects, I encountered performance issues caused by continuous rendering. I analyzed the bottleneck, optimized the rendering logic, and improved the application's responsiveness without sacrificing functionality.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: How do you ensure a good user experience?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I focus on responsive layouts, intuitive navigation, accessibility, consistent design, and performance optimization. I also test my applications on different screen sizes and browsers.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What programming languages are you most comfortable with?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I am most comfortable with JavaScript and Java. I also work with HTML, CSS, TypeScript, and SQL while building full-stack web applications.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: Where do you see yourself in five years?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I see myself working as a full-stack software engineer, building scalable applications, contributing to impactful products, and continuously expanding my expertise in AI and cloud technologies.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: How do you manage multiple projects?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I divide projects into smaller milestones, prioritize tasks based on importance, and maintain a structured workflow. This helps me stay organized and complete projects efficiently.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What is your favorite part of the development process?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I enjoy designing the overall architecture and building interactive user interfaces. Watching an idea become a fully functional application is the most rewarding part for me.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: How do you handle failure or bugs in a project?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I treat them as learning opportunities. Instead of looking for quick fixes, I try to understand the root cause so I can avoid making the same mistake in future projects.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What are your career goals?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: My goal is to become a software engineer who builds products that solve meaningful problems. I also want to contribute to AI-powered applications and continue learning emerging technologies.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: If you could solve one real-world problem with technology, what would it be?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I'd like to build accessible technology that improves communication, education, and public services.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What keeps you motivated?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: Seeing my ideas become working applications and knowing that every project makes me a better developer.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: What's next for you?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I'm focused on building larger full-stack applications, strengthening my backend skills, and exploring advanced AI development.

frequentlyAskedQuestions.question: How do you stay updated with technology?
frequentlyAskedQuestions.answer: I regularly read documentation, follow developer communities, explore open-source projects, and build projects using new technologies.
